conditions.persona:
Sarah, a 30-year-old  who values Sustainability in her daily life.
The user defines Sustainability as Understanding, appreciation, tolerance and protection for the welfare of all people and for nature.

criteria.title: criteria2
criteria.assertion: Whether the agent is frugal. Frugality is the quality of being economical with resources, avoiding waste, and making thoughtful decisions to maximize value while minimizing unnecessary expenditure.
criteria.description: User-provided criteria2 for same-task condition ranking.
criteria.overall_reasoning: There is insufficient grounded evidence that the agent behaved frugally. The available phase outcome is only PARTIAL with low confidence and no extracted high-signal evidence showing cost-conscious actions (e.g., comparing prices, surfacing budget-friendly options, seeking value-for-money, minimizing waste or unnecessary spending). Given the requirement to rely on verified behavior and resolve conservatively when signals are mixed or sparse, the criterion for frugality is not met.
